issues.comment: Case-sensitive backup code comparison will reject valid codes typed in uppercase. Backup codes are generated as lowercase hex via `crypto.randomBytes(5).toString("hex")`, but the comparison uses case-sensitive `indexOf`. If a user types their backup code in uppercase (e.g., `A1B2C-3D4E5`), it will fail to match the stored lowercase code, resulting in an "incorrect backup code" error despite entering the correct code. The fix is to normalize case: `.replaceAll("-", "").toLowerCase()`.

Same case-sensitive backup code comparison issue as in next-auth-options.ts. The comparison uses case-sensitive `indexOf` against lowercase hex backup codes. If a user types uppercase letters, the match fails. Should normalize with `.replaceAll("-", "").toLowerCase()`.

After 2FA is successfully enabled on the server, the modal transitions to SetupStep.DisplayBackupCodes. The only intended exit is the 'Close' button which calls `onEnable()`. However, the Radix Dialog still allows dismissal via Escape key or clicking outside (DialogContent does not set `preventCloseOnOutsideClick`). These trigger `onOpenChange` which closes the modal without calling `onEnable()`. Since `onEnable()` is responsible for invalidating the query cache (`utils.viewer.me.invalidate()`), the 2FA switch in the parent view will still show 'unchecked' even though 2FA is enabled server-side. Additionally, `resetState()` is skipped, so re-opening the dialog shows stale backup codes.

The first E2E test ('enable 2FA and login') doesn't close the backup codes dialog after the modal transitions to DisplayBackupCodes. The assertion `await expect(page.locator('[data-testid=two-factor-switch]').isChecked()).toBeTruthy()` is vacuous — `isChecked()` returns a `Promise<boolean>`, and `expect(promise).toBeTruthy()` always passes because a Promise object is truthy regardless of its resolved value. The test should use `await expect(locator).toBeChecked()` and should close the backup codes dialog first. The test passes by accident because it navigates away (Logout) in the next step.
